title: Marine Le Pen, phoenix of French far right, embarks on Elysée race
subhead: Marine Le Pen announced she would run for president hours after her embezzlement conviction was upheld.

title: Two Israeli NGOs warn of a 'de facto annexation at an unprecedented pace' in the West Bank
On day 1 of summit in Ankara, NATO unveils defense contracts worth billions
Péter Magyar launches contested procedure to remove Hungary's president
subhead: On July 7, the Hungarian parliament began to review a package of constitutional amendments that would remove President Tamas Sulyok, an ally of former prime minister Viktor Orban. This 'forceful' approach has drawn concern from several NGOs and advocates for the rule of law.

title: Hope of finding survivors after Venezuela quakes give way to mourning
subhead: After the twin earthquakes of June 24, the chances of finding anyone alive beneath the rubble are now extremely slim. Amid the devastated landscape, Venezuelan photographer Alejandro Cegarra documented for Le Monde the search efforts taking place in the ruins and how Venezuelan society has organized itself in the wake of the disaster.

title: Quebec's 'moving day' is overshadowed by housing crisis
subhead: In a unique tradition in the Canadian province, tens of thousands of people move on the same day, July 1. But in recent years, this ritual has been marred by serious difficulties in finding new housing.

title: Albert Camus archives, including manuscripts, forged documents and letters, join national collection
subhead: The archives, previously kept within the writer's family, has been acquired by France's national library for €9 million with support from Hermès and CIC.

subhead: Two journalists from the 'New York Times' retrace the early months of the US president's second term, highlighting the ruptures and upheavals his approach to power has unleashed.

title: Pyrénées wildfires destroy everything in their path: 'I didn't think I'd make it out alive'
subhead: The residents of Ille-sur-Têt, a town that was evacuated, remain in shock as the Trévillach wildfire – the largest the region has seen in 50 years – was still not under control on Monday evening.

title: One hundred early works by Claude Monet reunited at Le Havre's André Malraux Museum
subhead: The MuMa exhibition, showcasing drawings, caricatures and paintings, places the painter in his family and regional context.
